senses.definition: God's title, given and used by Christians
the man based on whose teachings Christianity is established

examples.x: Christians believe that Christ died for the sins of humanity and rose from the dead.
The teachings of Christ emphasize love, forgiveness, and compassion.
During the Christmas season, many people celebrate the birth of Christ.
The apostles spread the message of Christ throughout the ancient world.
The Sermon on the Mount is one of the most famous discourses given by Christ.

senses.definition: the man whose followers believe to be the son of God and whose teachings are the foundation of the Christian religion

examples.x: Jesus taught his followers to love their neighbors as themselves.
The story of Jesus' birth is a central part of the Christmas tradition.
Many people seek guidance and inspiration from the words and actions of Jesus.
Jesus performed many miracles, including healing the sick and turning water into wine.
The resurrection of Jesus is celebrated by Christians around the world on Easter Sunday.

senses.definition: someone who, after their death, is officially recognized by the Christian Church as a very holy person
the person who leads the Roman Catholic Church

examples.x: The Pope addressed the crowd from the balcony of St. Peter's Basilica.
Catholics around the world look to the Pope for spiritual guidance and leadership.
During his visit, the Pope met with various religious and political leaders to discuss global issues.
The election of a new Pope is a significant event, followed closely by millions.
The Pope issued an encyclical calling for action on climate change and social justice.

senses.definition: a high-ranking priest who supervises all the churches and priests in a city

examples.x: The bishop presided over the ordination ceremony for the new priests in the diocese.
During the annual meeting, the bishop addressed concerns from various parishes in the city.
The cathedral, being the seat of the bishop, hosted several important religious events throughout the year.
The bishop met with community leaders to discuss ways the church could support local social programs.
After years of dedicated service, he was appointed bishop and given responsibility for overseeing all the churches in the city.

senses.definition: a priest or minister who is in charge of a church

examples.x: The pastor delivered a powerful sermon that resonated with the entire congregation.
As the church's pastor, she provided guidance and support to her parishioners in times of need.
The pastor organized community outreach programs to help those in need.
After the service, the pastor greeted everyone and offered words of encouragement.
The pastor spent years studying theology and serving in various capacities before leading his own church.

senses.definition: a man who is a member of a religious group, particularly a monk

examples.x: The abbey is home to several brothers who have devoted their lives to spiritual practice.
The brothers of the order take vows of poverty, chastity, and obedience.

senses.definition: someone who is sent to a foreign country to teach and talk about religion, particularly to persuade others to become a member of the Christian Church

examples.x: The missionary spent years in remote villages, sharing the teachings of Christianity with the local people.
During his time abroad, the missionary learned the local language to better communicate with the community.
The church raised funds to support the missionary in his work across different countries.

senses.definition: a member of a male religious group that lives in a monastery

examples.x: The monk spent his days in prayer and contemplation within the monastery walls.
As a monk, he took vows of poverty, chastity, and obedience to live a life dedicated to God.
Monks often engage in manual labor as part of their daily routine to support their community.
The monastery welcomed visitors who sought spiritual guidance from the monks.
The monk's robe and shaved head were symbols of his commitment to his religious order.

senses.definition: a member of a female religious group that lives in a convent

examples.x: The nun devoted her life to prayer and service within the convent.
As a nun, she took vows of poverty, chastity, and obedience to live a life of religious devotion.
Nuns often engage in teaching, healthcare, and charitable works as part of their ministry.
The convent provided a peaceful retreat for the nuns to focus on their spiritual growth.
The nun's habit and veil were symbols of her commitment to her religious community.

senses.definition: related to or belonging to the Western branch of the Christian Church that is led by the Pope

examples.x: The Catholic tradition places significant importance on the intercession of saints.

senses.definition: related to or belonging to the Western branch of the Christian Church, distinct from the Roman Catholic Church

examples.x: He grew up in a Protestant household, attending church regularly with his family.
The Protestant tradition emphasizes the authority of the Bible in matters of faith and practice.
She participated in Protestant youth group activities during her teenage years.

senses.definition: a group of people who gather in a church to say prayers

examples.x: The congregation gathered every Sunday for worship and fellowship.
The minister addressed the congregation, delivering a heartfelt message of hope.
Members of the congregation volunteered to help with community outreach programs.
The choir sang hymns that uplifted the spirits of the congregation.
The congregation celebrated Easter together with a joyful service and shared meal.

senses.definition: an area with a church of its own that is under the care of a priest

examples.x: The parish is known for its strong sense of community and active involvement in local charities.
Each parish organizes events and activities to foster unity among its members.
The parish hosted a festival to celebrate its patron saint, drawing attendees from neighboring communities.
The parish encompasses several neighborhoods, each with its own unique history and traditions.
The parish celebrated its centennial anniversary with a special Mass and community picnic.

senses.definition: someone who has changed their religion or opinion regarding a matter

examples.x: He was raised in a different faith but became a devout convert to Christianity in his adult years.
The community welcomed the new converts with open arms during the baptism ceremony.
She is a passionate advocate for animal rights, having been a convert to the cause after witnessing animal cruelty firsthand.
The group of converts met regularly to study the teachings of their newly adopted religion.
The convert shared their journey of spiritual discovery with fellow believers in a heartfelt testimony.

senses.definition: someone who respects, supports, and believes in a certain individual or system of ideas

examples.x: He was a devoted follower of Buddha, practicing meditation daily.
The preacher's sermon attracted many followers who sought spiritual guidance.
As a follower of Gandhi's teachings, she believed in nonviolent resistance as a force for change.
The religious leader attracted thousands of followers to his sermons and teachings.

senses.definition: a religious person who travels to a sacred place for a holy cause

examples.x: Every year, thousands of pilgrims travel to Mecca for the Hajj pilgrimage.
The pilgrims walked the Camino de Santiago, seeking spiritual renewal and reflection.
She joined a group of pilgrims on a journey to Lourdes, hoping for healing and solace.
The ancient temple attracts pilgrims from all over the world who come to pay their respects.
As a pilgrim, he embraced the challenges of the journey as part of his spiritual growth.

senses.definition: a Christian religious ceremony during which a baby is named and admitted to the Christian Church

examples.x: The christening ceremony was held in the local church, with family and friends in attendance.
They chose a beautiful gown for the baby to wear during the christening.
The priest conducted the christening, pouring water over the baby's forehead as a symbol of purification.
After the christening, there was a reception at their home to celebrate the occasion.
The godparents played an important role in the christening, promising to support the child in their spiritual journey.

senses.definition: a prayer asking for God's protection and help

examples.x: The priest offered a heartfelt blessing for peace and prosperity in the community.
Before the meal, the family gathered around the table to say a blessing.
The couple received a special blessing from the bishop on their wedding day.
She felt a sense of peace and comfort after receiving the priest's blessing.
The grandmother gave her grandchildren her blessing before they embarked on their journey abroad.

senses.definition: someone who follows Buddhism

examples.x: She became a Buddhist after studying the teachings of Siddhartha Gautama.

senses.definition: a religious ceremony in which people bury or cremate a dead person

examples.x: The funeral was held at the church where she had been an active member for many years.
Family and friends gathered to pay their respects at the funeral service.
The priest delivered a comforting eulogy during the funeral.
After the funeral, there was a reception to share memories and support one another.
The funeral procession made its way to the cemetery, where she was laid to rest beside her husband.

senses.definition: a group of people with extreme religious views who are separate from any established religion

examples.x: The authorities investigated allegations of abuse within the cult.
She was drawn into a cult during her vulnerable teenage years.
The documentary exposed the secretive practices of the cult.
Members of the cult isolated themselves from society to maintain their beliefs.
After leaving the cult, she sought counseling to recover from the psychological impact of her experience.

senses.definition: the power that some people believe controls everything that occurs and that is inevitable

examples.x: She believed in the power of fate to guide her life's journey, trusting that everything happens for a reason.
Despite their best efforts, they felt powerless against the dictates of fate.
The ancient Greeks believed that fate was determined by the gods, shaping the destinies of mortals.
He pondered the concept of fate, wondering if it was predestined or subject to free will.
In literature, characters often grapple with the idea of fate, questioning whether they can alter their destinies.

senses.definition: the religion of the Muslims, which was established by Muhammad whose holy book is called the Quran

examples.x: Islam is one of the world's major religions, with millions of followers around the globe.
Muslims follow the teachings of Islam, which include the Five Pillars as guidelines for faith and practice.
The Quran is the holy book of Islam, believed to be the word of God as revealed to Muhammad.
During Ramadan, Muslims fast from dawn to sunset as an act of devotion and spiritual reflection in accordance with Islam.
Islam teaches compassion, charity, and justice as fundamental values in daily life.

senses.definition: the Arab prophet who established the religion of Islam

examples.x: Muhammad is considered the founder of Islam and the last prophet in the religion.
Muslims regard Muhammad as the messenger of God who received revelations through the angel Gabriel.
The life of Muhammad is documented in the Quran and Hadith, providing guidance for Muslims.
Muhammad's teachings emphasize monotheism, morality, and social justice.
The city of Mecca holds special significance as the birthplace of Muhammad and the holiest site in Islam.

senses.definition: a building where a group of monks live and pray

examples.x: The monastery nestled in the mountains provided a serene environment for meditation and prayer.
Monks in the monastery follow a strict daily schedule of work, study, and worship.
Visitors to the monastery are often impressed by its beautiful architecture and tranquil gardens.
The monastery is known for its centuries-old manuscripts and religious artifacts.
The abbot of the monastery oversees its spiritual and administrative matters.

senses.definition: a place or building for people to pray in, which is considered holy by many due to its connection with a sacred person, event, or object

examples.x: Pilgrims from around the world visit the shrine to pay their respects and seek blessings.
The shrine is a place of pilgrimage where believers gather to pray and offer offerings.
The ancient shrine houses relics believed to have healing powers.
The local community maintains the shrine as a sacred place of worship and reflection.
The shrine attracts thousands of devotees during religious festivals and special occasions.

senses.definition: the act of conducting a series of fixed actions, particular to a religious ceremony
originating from, relating to, or associated with God or a god

examples.x: The ancient Greeks believed in divine beings residing on Mount Olympus.
Many people find comfort in divine intervention during times of trouble.
She felt a sense of divine presence while meditating in nature.
The priestess received divine inspiration through visions and dreams.

senses.definition: connected with God or a god, and considered holy or deeply respected in religious contexts

examples.x: The river is considered sacred in many indigenous cultures.
The sacred rituals performed during the ceremony were deeply meaningful.
The temple is a sacred place of worship for Hindus.
The sacred symbols adorning the shrine hold spiritual significance for believers.

senses.definition: not concerned or connected with religion

examples.x: The school is secular, meaning it does not promote any specific religious beliefs.
The country's legal system is based on secular principles, separate from religious doctrine.
She identifies as secular, preferring to approach life without relying on religious beliefs.
Secular holidays celebrate events or traditions that are not rooted in religious practices.
Secular organizations advocate for the separation of church and state in public affairs.

senses.definition: to give a religious speech, particularly in a church

examples.x: Every Sunday, the pastor preaches sermons to the congregation, sharing biblical wisdom.
The priest was invited to preach during the special Easter service, focusing on the message of resurrection.
The priest will preach on Sunday about forgiveness and compassion.
The pastor preached a powerful sermon that inspired the whole community.

senses.definition: to kill an animal or person as a religious act
to act against religious or moral rules

examples.x: They believed it was wrong to sin against their neighbors by spreading false rumors.
The priest reminded the congregation not to sin by harboring hatred in their hearts.
According to their beliefs, to sin is to go against the teachings of their faith.
He struggled with the temptation to sin but ultimately chose to uphold his moral values.

senses.definition: to respect and honor God or a deity, especially by performing rituals

examples.x: During the festival, devotees gather at the temple to worship the deity with offerings of flowers, incense, and fruits.
The priest led the ceremony, guiding the worshippers in rituals to worship the goddess of fertility and abundance.
The community gathered at the sacred grove to worship the nature spirits and offer thanks for the harvest.
In traditional cultures, people would gather at the sacred stone circle to worship the sun god and celebrate the changing seasons.
The followers worship their god through daily prayers and ceremonies.

senses.definition: a bishop of the highest rank who is responsible for all the churches in a specific large area

examples.x: The archbishop presided over the diocese, which encompassed several cities and towns.
As an archbishop, he played a key role in shaping the direction of the church in his region.
The archbishop conducted the ordination ceremony for new priests with solemnity and grace.
He sought counsel from the archbishop on matters of faith and community outreach.
The cathedral hosted a special Mass to celebrate the anniversary of the archbishop's ordination.

senses.definition: very faithful to God and devoted to religion
lacking faith in or respect for God

examples.x: The godless society depicted in the novel had abandoned all forms of religious belief.
He accused her of leading a godless life devoid of moral principles.
The preacher warned his congregation about the dangers of living in a godless world.
Despite growing up in a godless household, she eventually found solace in spirituality.
The godless regime persecuted anyone practicing religion openly.

senses.definition: to show deep respect and admiration for God

examples.x: They were taught to fear God and follow His teachings.
The scriptures remind us to fear the Lord with all our hearts.
To fear God is to recognize His greatness and authority.
Fearing God, he chose to act with honesty and kindness.
She believed that fearing God brought wisdom and strength.
